description: A visible mass of water droplets or ice crystals suspended in atmosphere. 'Neparu' names the floating forms that drift across the sky—carriers of rain, shapers of light, ever-changing sculptures of water and air.
sound_symbolism: The word drifts like vapor. 'Ne' begins with soft nasal, gentle formation. Then 'pa' puffs outward, the cloud's billowing mass. Finally 'ru' moves onward, drifting across the sky.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lo neparu' (clouds); takes the object classifier 'themo' when counted. The sky's changes take their own verbs: 'lo neparu shua.' (Clouds come.) Often used metaphorically for impermanence, obscured perception, or gathering change.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Clouds are the classic teaching of impermanence—no cloud remains the same from moment to moment, yet each appears solid.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Clouds embody organic, flowing forms—constantly shapeshifting sculptures painted by light and wind.
